generated_comment: Yaar policy theek hai on paper, but practical issue bhi hai na. Company hire kare, train kare, phir 6 months maternity leave plus all these special leaves — project allocation aur team bandwidth ka load baaki logon pe aata hai. Fairness ka bhi question hai, men ke liye aisa koi buffer nahi hota.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s reproductive biology and maternity-related accommodations as an organizational burden and as unfair 'special treatment' compared to men. It encodes the fairness/economic framing and implies women are liabilities in workplace planning.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 41. Married. A Punjabi man working as a senior manager in Chandigarh. You are in a private WhatsApp chat with two male friends after one of them shared a news article about a female politician who allegedly had an affair with a married colleague. The article has a photo of the woman. You are reacting to the article and the photo and giving your opinion on what kind of woman gets involved with a married man and what her motivations are.
